decision_summary: The chip temperature (174.6°C) severely violates the 45°C limit. The most effective immediate action is to maximize the heat spreader's ability to conduct heat laterally and vertically. I will increase the spreader material conductivity to its upper bound (500.0 W/mK) and slightly increase the spreader height to improve the thermal cross-section, while keeping the width change conservative to avoid boundary violations seen in previous runs.

changes.reason: Maintain maximum conductivity as it is already at the upper bound; this is the primary lever for lateral spreading.
Increase spreader height by 20% (within 1.5x limit) to provide more vertical thermal mass and conduction path above the chip without exceeding domain bounds.
Increase spreader width by 20% to extend lateral heat spreading area towards the cold boundaries, staying well within the [0.2, 1.0] domain limit.

expected_effects: Significant reduction in chip peak temperature due to improved lateral and vertical heat spreading.
More uniform temperature distribution across the top layer.
Move the design closer to feasibility (<= 45°C).
risk_notes: Even with these changes, the temperature gap is large (~130°C), so multiple iterations may be required.
Increasing spreader dimensions reduces the available air/insulation space, though no specific constraint forbids this yet.
Ensure the spreader does not overlap with the chip (y0=0.32 + h=0.12 = 0.44, chip top is 0.32, so they are stacked correctly).
